 `./pdr/003-claude-md-update-propagation.md`

```text
1	# PDR-003 — CLAUDE.md update propagation
2	
3	**Status:** Proposed
4	**Date:** 2026-05-14
5	**Author:** lead-po (subagent dispatch)
6	**Beads:** lead-5kn
7	
8	## Context
9	
10	A shop must bootstrap and run self-contained — the `repos/` directory of
11	the lead-shop product is not assumed available inside a bootstrapped shop.
12	That was the bootstrap-time framing. The **temporal corollary** has not
13	been pinned by scenarios: once bootstrapped, a shop must stay self-
14	contained as the canonical evolves. Concretely, when the canonical
15	`CLAUDE.md` primer in `shopsystem-templates` changes (e.g., the Monitor-
16	arming directive added by scenarios 71/72/73), bootstrapped shops must
17	receive that change through `shop-templates update`, not through manual
18	re-editing in each shop.
19	
20	The current contract pins the **opposite**: `shop-templates update` is
21	explicitly forbidden to touch the target's `CLAUDE.md`, even when the
22	canonical primer has changed since bootstrap (scenario 39, reinforced by
23	scenario 56's error-path invariant). The source comment at
24	`repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/cli.py:585-587` cites four
25	hashes as the "non-touch invariant on CLAUDE.md / .gitignore / .beads/".
26	
27	The deeper tension is that `CLAUDE.md` is **part-canonical, part-per-
28	shop**: scenario 45 requires the bootstrap-generated `CLAUDE.md` to name
29	the shop's own identity (shop name, role set), while scenario 44 holds
30	the canonical primer as package-data source-of-truth. Empirical evidence:
31	this product's `repos/shopsystem-templates/CLAUDE.md` carries canonical
32	primer text *and* shop-authored sections like "Repo layout" and "Build &
33	Test" that no canonical template would dictate. Any propagation contract
34	must reconcile these.
35	
36	## Design alternatives
37	
38	### A. Full replace
39	`update` overwrites `CLAUDE.md` from canonical unconditionally. Cleanest
40	mechanism; loses scenario 45 (no shop identity in the result) and erases
41	shop-authored prose. Rejected.
42	
43	### B. Identity parameterization
44	`CLAUDE.md` becomes fully canonical once shop name and role set are
45	template parameters resolved at update time. The shop owns *no*
46	`CLAUDE.md` content. Pure mechanism, but requires that no shop ever
47	benefits from local primer additions — empirically false for the
48	`shopsystem-templates` BC repo today, which carries shop-authored "Repo
49	layout" and "Build & Test" sections. Would force migration of all
50	shop-authored prose elsewhere before adoption. Rejected as the first
51	move; viable as a longer-term direction once the canonical primer is
52	parameterized.
53	
54	### C. Sentinel-delimited canonical region
55	The canonical primer template defines a region delimited by sentinel
56	comments (e.g., `<!-- shop-templates:canonical:begin -->` and
57	`<!-- shop-templates:canonical:end -->`). `update` replaces only the
58	text between sentinels in the target's `CLAUDE.md`; everything outside
59	the sentinels is preserved byte-for-byte. Requires mutating `CLAUDE.md`
60	on every update, which contradicts scenario 39's byte-for-byte non-touch
61	invariant and forces amending scenarios 39 and 56 to scope the invariant
62	to outside-sentinel content. Rejected in favor of E (below), which
63	achieves the same propagation outcome without contradicting any existing
64	scenario.
65	
66	### D. Sectioned merge by header name
67	Like C but keyed on Markdown headers instead of sentinels. Rejected as
68	fragile: header text edits in either the canonical template or the
69	target would silently break the merge contract. Sentinels are explicit;
70	headers are not.
71	
72	### E. Canonical primer in dedicated imported files (RECOMMENDED)
73	The canonical primer content lives in a dedicated file (or files) under
74	a bootstrap-managed directory. `CLAUDE.md` becomes **shop-owned end to
75	end**: it contains shop-specific preamble (identity, repo layout, shop-
76	authored prose) and uses Claude Code's `@<filename>` import directive
77	to pull the canonical primer content into the assistant's context at
78	session start. `update` overwrites the canonical imported file(s) in
79	place and never touches `CLAUDE.md`.
80	
81	Path convention (this PDR's choice): canonical imported files live under
82	`.claude/canonical/`. The lead-shop primer is
83	`.claude/canonical/lead-primer.md`; the BC-shop primer is
84	`.claude/canonical/bc-primer.md`. The directory is bootstrap-managed
85	and symmetric across shop types. It is distinct from `.claude/agents/`
86	(subagent role prompts) and `.claude/settings.json` (Claude Code
87	configuration), so there is no namespace collision. It is created and
88	populated by `bootstrap` and rewritten in place by `update`. It survives
89	`bd init` and `git init` because it is a regular directory under
90	`.claude/` with no special tooling claim on it.
91	
92	Pros:
93	- Scenario 39's byte-for-byte non-touch invariant on `CLAUDE.md` is
94	  **preserved unchanged** — `update` literally does not open
95	  `CLAUDE.md` for writing. Scenario 56's error-path clause on
96	  `CLAUDE.md` is likewise preserved unchanged.
97	- Canonical content has a dedicated home, cleanly separated from shop-
98	  authored prose. No parsing of `CLAUDE.md` is required by `update`.
99	- `update`'s mechanism for the canonical primer is a pure file write
100	  — symmetric with how `update` handles the role-prompt files under
101	  `.claude/agents/` today. No merge logic, no diff regions, no
102	  precedence rules.
103	- Adding new canonical files later (e.g., a per-shop-type ADR digest)
104	  is additive and does not require schema changes to `CLAUDE.md`.
105	
106	Cons:
107	- Depends on Claude Code's `@<filename>` import mechanism being stable
108	  and being resolved correctly by every consumer (CLI, IDE extension,
109	  any future agent runtime that reads `CLAUDE.md`). The user named the
110	  mechanism explicitly, so it exists; the syntax should be confirmed
111	  against authoritative Claude Code documentation before the
112	  Implementer pins literal text in step definitions.
113	- Adds an indirection: a human reading `CLAUDE.md` must follow the
114	  import to see the full router contract. The shop-owned `CLAUDE.md`
115	  should make the import visible (top of file) and name what the
116	  imported file contributes.
117	- Migration: shops bootstrapped under the old contract (this product's
118	  own lead-shop included) have the canonical primer text inlined into
119	  `CLAUDE.md` and have no `.claude/canonical/` directory. `update` must
120	  handle this case explicitly — see migration scenario below.
121	
122	## Recommendation
123	
124	Adopt **alternative E — canonical primer in dedicated imported files**.
125	The contract becomes:
126	
127	1. Canonical primer content for each shop type ships as a dedicated
128	   file in `shop-templates` package data (`lead-primer.md`,
129	   `bc-primer.md`), parallel to the role-prompt template files already
130	   shipped there.
131	2. Bootstrap writes that canonical file into the target shop at
132	   `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md`, and writes a `CLAUDE.md`
133	   whose top content includes a Claude Code `@<filename>` import line
134	   referencing the canonical file. Shop identity content (scenario 45)
135	   lives in the shop-owned `CLAUDE.md` body, outside the import.
136	3. `update` overwrites `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md` in
137	   place when the canonical has drifted from the target's copy; it
138	   does not open or touch `CLAUDE.md`.
139	4. `update` is idempotent when the target's canonical file already
140	   matches package data.
141	5. Migration: if `update` runs in a shop that lacks
142	   `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md` (legacy bootstrap, including
143	   this product's own lead shop today), `update` **creates** the file
144	   from canonical and emits a visible diagnostic naming the follow-up
145	   the operator must perform manually — namely, removing the now-
146	   duplicated inlined primer text from `CLAUDE.md` and adding the
147	   `@<filename>` import line. `update` does **not** edit `CLAUDE.md`
148	   automatically, preserving scenario 39. The diagnostic must be
149	   distinguishable from the success case (non-zero exit or a clearly-
150	   marked WARNING line — to be settled when the Implementer pins the
151	   exact UX).
152	
153	## Scenarios this PDR contradicts
154	
155	None. Alternative E preserves every existing `CLAUDE.md`-related
156	invariant by moving the propagation surface off `CLAUDE.md` entirely.
157	
158	## Scenarios this PDR preserves (unchanged)
159	
160	- **39** (`update-preserves-shop-owned-claude-md-edits`) — byte-for-byte
161	  preservation of `CLAUDE.md` holds. `update` does not open `CLAUDE.md`
162	  for writing under E.
163	- **56** (`update-rejects-missing-target-with-argparse-error`) — the
164	  error-path clause that `CLAUDE.md` is unchanged from before the
165	  invocation holds verbatim.
166	- **45** (shop identity in bootstrap-generated `CLAUDE.md`) — shop
167	  identity content lives in the shop-owned `CLAUDE.md` body, written
168	  by `bootstrap`, outside any import.
169	- **44** (canonical primer is package data) — preserved; the canonical
170	  primer continues to ship as package data, now as dedicated
171	  `<shop-type>-primer.md` files.
172	- **31** (bootstrap writes top-level `CLAUDE.md`) — preserved;
173	  `bootstrap` continues to write `CLAUDE.md`, and additionally writes
174	  `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md`.
175	- **40, 41** (`.gitignore` and `.beads/` non-touch invariants) —
176	  unaffected.
177	
178	## Scenarios this PDR amends (Architect call)
179	
180	- **71 / 72 / 73** (Monitor-arming directive in canonical and
181	  bootstrap-generated `CLAUDE.md`). Under E, the directive's storage
182	  location moves: it lives in `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md`
183	  (the canonical file) and is reachable from `CLAUDE.md` via the
184	  import. There are two viable phrasings, and the Architect should
185	  pick one:
186	  - **Phrasing A (recommended):** retain the scenarios as-is, on the
187	    interpretation that "the bootstrap-generated `CLAUDE.md`" denotes
188	    the assistant-resolved `CLAUDE.md` (imports included). This keeps
189	    the assertion at the level of observable agent behavior, which is
190	    what the scenarios are pinning.
191	  - **Phrasing B:** amend the scenarios to assert the directive lives
192	    in the canonical primer file specifically, and that `CLAUDE.md`
193	    imports it. This is more mechanism-precise but couples the
194	    scenarios to the import mechanism.
195	  PO leans toward Phrasing A — the scenarios pin a behavior (directive
196	  is present in the agent's startup context), not a storage location.
197	
198	## New scenarios this PDR will author after acceptance
199	
200	1. Canonical primer for each shop type ships as a dedicated file in
201	   `shop-templates` package data — `lead-primer.md` for lead shops,
202	   `bc-primer.md` for BC shops.
203	2. Bootstrap writes the canonical primer for the target shop type into
204	   `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md`.
205	3. Bootstrap writes a `CLAUDE.md` that imports the canonical primer file
206	   (property-level assertion: the canonical text reaches the assistant
207	   via import, not inlining). The literal import syntax will be settled
208	   against Claude Code documentation during implementation; scenarios
209	   should be authored to pin the property and flag the syntax as an
210	   Implementer follow-up.
211	4. `update` overwrites `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md` in
212	   place when the canonical has drifted from the target's copy.
213	5. `update` does not open or modify `CLAUDE.md` on the success path
214	   (reinforces scenario 39 from a different angle).
215	6. `update` is idempotent when the target's canonical primer file
216	   already matches package data — exit zero, no diagnostic, no file
217	   modification.
218	7. Migration scenario: when `update` runs in a shop that lacks
219	   `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md`, `update` creates the
220	   canonical file from package data, leaves `CLAUDE.md` untouched, and
221	   emits a visible diagnostic naming the manual follow-up (de-inline
222	   the primer from `CLAUDE.md` and add the import line).
223	
224	## Open question (for Architect)
225	
226	1. Phrasing A vs. Phrasing B above for scenarios 71/72/73.
227	2. The migration scenario's UX shape — non-zero exit vs. zero exit with
228	   a WARNING line. Both have precedent; the choice is the Architect's
229	   when composing the dispatch.
230	3. Whether the canonical-files directory `.claude/canonical/` is the
231	   right path (this PDR's call), or whether the Architect sees a
232	   stronger argument for an alternative location.
233
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 20:24:42Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```md
# PDR-003 — CLAUDE.md update propagation

**Status:** Proposed
**Date:** 2026-05-14
**Author:** lead-po (subagent dispatch)
**Beads:** lead-5kn

## Context

A shop must bootstrap and run self-contained — the `repos/` directory of
the lead-shop product is not assumed available inside a bootstrapped shop.
That was the bootstrap-time framing. The **temporal corollary** has not
been pinned by scenarios: once bootstrapped, a shop must stay self-
contained as the canonical evolves. Concretely, when the canonical
`CLAUDE.md` primer in `shopsystem-templates` changes (e.g., the Monitor-
arming directive added by scenarios 71/72/73), bootstrapped shops must
receive that change through `shop-templates update`, not through manual
re-editing in each shop.

The current contract pins the **opposite**: `shop-templates update` is
explicitly forbidden to touch the target's `CLAUDE.md`, even when the
canonical primer has changed since bootstrap (scenario 39, reinforced by
scenario 56's error-path invariant). The source comment at
`repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/cli.py:585-587` cites four
hashes as the "non-touch invariant on CLAUDE.md / .gitignore / .beads/".

The deeper tension is that `CLAUDE.md` is **part-canonical, part-per-
shop**: scenario 45 requires the bootstrap-generated `CLAUDE.md` to name
the shop's own identity (shop name, role set), while scenario 44 holds
the canonical primer as package-data source-of-truth. Empirical evidence:
this product's `repos/shopsystem-templates/CLAUDE.md` carries canonical
primer text *and* shop-authored sections like "Repo layout" and "Build &
Test" that no canonical template would dictate. Any propagation contract
must reconcile these.

## Design alternatives

### A. Full replace
`update` overwrites `CLAUDE.md` from canonical unconditionally. Cleanest
mechanism; loses scenario 45 (no shop identity in the result) and erases
shop-authored prose. Rejected.

### B. Identity parameterization
`CLAUDE.md` becomes fully canonical once shop name and role set are
template parameters resolved at update time. The shop owns *no*
`CLAUDE.md` content. Pure mechanism, but requires that no shop ever
benefits from local primer additions — empirically false for the
`shopsystem-templates` BC repo today, which carries shop-authored "Repo
layout" and "Build & Test" sections. Would force migration of all
shop-authored prose elsewhere before adoption. Rejected as the first
move; viable as a longer-term direction once the canonical primer is
parameterized.

### C. Sentinel-delimited canonical region
The canonical primer template defines a region delimited by sentinel
comments (e.g., `<!-- shop-templates:canonical:begin -->` and
`<!-- shop-templates:canonical:end -->`). `update` replaces only the
text between sentinels in the target's `CLAUDE.md`; everything outside
the sentinels is preserved byte-for-byte. Requires mutating `CLAUDE.md`
on every update, which contradicts scenario 39's byte-for-byte non-touch
invariant and forces amending scenarios 39 and 56 to scope the invariant
to outside-sentinel content. Rejected in favor of F (below); F achieves
the same propagation outcome with cleaner ownership and no merge logic.

### D. Sectioned merge by header name
Like C but keyed on Markdown headers instead of sentinels. Rejected as
fragile: header text edits in either the canonical template or the
target would silently break the merge contract. Sentinels are explicit;
headers are not.

### E. Canonical primer in dedicated imported files, with shop-owned `CLAUDE.md`
The canonical primer content lives in a dedicated file under
`.claude/canonical/`. `CLAUDE.md` is shop-owned end to end and imports
the canonical file via Claude Code's `@<filename>` directive. `update`
overwrites the canonical imported file in place and never touches
`CLAUDE.md`. Preserves scenarios 39, 45, 56 verbatim. Rejected per
stakeholder directive (2026-05-14) in favor of F, which trades some
scenario preservation for a cleaner ownership story.

### F. Canonical-managed `CLAUDE.md` with four typed imports (RECOMMENDED)
`CLAUDE.md` is **canonical-managed and overwritten by `update`**. Its
body is intentionally minimal: a short header plus a fixed sequence of
`@<filename>` imports. The substantive content lives in four typed
files, each with a single owner:

1. **Shop-name file** — contains the shop's own name (the literal string
   passed as `--shop-name` at bootstrap). **Bootstrap-only**, never
   touched by `update`. Owner: the shop.
2. **Shop-type file** — contains the shop's type token (`bc` or `lead`).
   **Bootstrap-only**, never touched by `update`. Owner: the shop.
3. **Canonical primer file** — the canonical router-prompt content for
   this shop type. Ships as package data; **overwritten by `update`** on
   drift. Owner: canonical (`shop-templates` package data).
4. **Shop-specific primer file** — shop-authored prose (e.g., "Repo
   layout", "Build & Test", anything the shop wants to add). Created
   at bootstrap as an empty (or near-empty) placeholder. **Never touched
   by `update`**. Owner: the shop.

`CLAUDE.md` itself is a deterministic function of canonical package data
and the shop type — the same `CLAUDE.md` body is written into every shop
of a given type. Bootstrap parameters (`--shop-name`, `--shop-type`) are
captured in the two shop-owned dotfiles imported by `CLAUDE.md`, not
spliced into `CLAUDE.md` itself.

Pros:
- **Cleanest ownership story.** Every file has exactly one owner. The
  path itself signals ownership (see "File-path convention" below). A
  reader can answer "who writes this file?" without reading its
  contents.
- **Canonical content propagation is unconditional and bulletproof.**
  The shop cannot accidentally remove an import or hand-edit `CLAUDE.md`
  in a way that loses canonical content, because `update` rewrites
  `CLAUDE.md` from canonical every time. No drift is possible.
- **No merge logic, no sentinels, no parsing.** `update` performs four
  operations at most: rewrite `CLAUDE.md`, rewrite the canonical primer
  file, leave the two shop-owned files alone, leave the shop-specific
  primer alone.
- **Shop-authored prose has a dedicated home.** The shop-specific
  primer file is the named, expected place for "Repo layout", "Build &
  Test", and other shop-local content. Separation of concerns is
  explicit at the file boundary, not buried inside a multi-author file.
- **Symmetric with how `update` already handles `.claude/agents/`.** Both
  surfaces become "canonical package data poured in place"; `CLAUDE.md`
  joins the same model rather than being a special case.

Cons:
- **Scenario 39 is contradicted.** `update` does overwrite `CLAUDE.md`.
  Operators who hand-edited `CLAUDE.md` under the old contract lose
  those edits on `update`. A migration story is required (see below).
  The recommendation accepts this hit deliberately; the cleaner
  ownership story is judged to be worth the cost.
- **Four files instead of one.** More bootstrap surface; a new reader
  has more files to orient to. Mitigated by `CLAUDE.md` being short and
  literally naming each import (e.g., `@.claude/shop/name.md  # the
  shop's own name`) so the import graph is self-documenting.
- **Depends on Claude Code's `@<filename>` import mechanism** being
  stable across consumers (CLI, IDE extension, future agent runtimes).
  Same dependency as alternative E.
- **Bootstrap parameter capture lives in a file, not in prose.** A
  human glancing at `CLAUDE.md` will see only the imports, not the
  shop's name directly. They must follow `@.claude/shop/name.md` to
  see "shopsystem-product". Mitigated by `CLAUDE.md` placing the
  shop-identity imports at the top, so the indirection is visible
  immediately.

## Recommendation

Adopt **alternative F — canonical-managed `CLAUDE.md` with four typed
imports**. The contract becomes:

1. Canonical primer content for each shop type ships as a dedicated file
   in `shop-templates` package data (`lead-primer.md`, `bc-primer.md`),
   parallel to the role-prompt template files already shipped there. A
   canonical `CLAUDE.md` template body also ships as package data, one
   per shop type.
2. Bootstrap writes **five** files into the target shop:
   - `CLAUDE.md` at the target root (canonical-managed).
   - `.claude/shop/name.md` (shop-owned, bootstrap-only) — the literal
     value passed as `--shop-name`.
   - `.claude/shop/type.md` (shop-owned, bootstrap-only) — the literal
     value passed as `--shop-type`.
   - `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md` (canonical-managed) —
     the canonical router-prompt body.
   - `.claude/shop/primer.md` (shop-owned, bootstrap-only placeholder)
     — empty or near-empty; the shop fills it in over time.
   `CLAUDE.md`'s body imports those four files in a fixed order with
   short headings that name each import's role.
3. `update` overwrites two files: `CLAUDE.md` (from the canonical
   `CLAUDE.md` template for the shop's declared type) and
   `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md` (from the canonical primer
   for the shop's declared type). It reads `.claude/shop/type.md` to
   know which shop type's canonical to apply.
4. `update` is idempotent when both canonical-managed files already
   match package data byte-for-byte — no diagnostic, no file mtime
   change, exit zero.
5. `update` does **not** touch `.claude/shop/name.md`,
   `.claude/shop/type.md`, or `.claude/shop/primer.md` under any
   circumstances.
6. **Migration** for legacy shops (including this product's own lead
   shop today, whose `CLAUDE.md` predates the four-file split): see the
   "Migration" subsection below.

### File-path convention

The split lives under `.claude/`:

- `.claude/canonical/` — **canonical-managed**. Files here are written
  by `bootstrap` and rewritten by `update` from package data. Operators
  must not hand-edit; edits will be silently reverted on the next
  `update`. Today this directory already houses the canonical agent
  files (`bc-implementer.md`, etc., via `.claude/agents/` — see note
  below); alternative F adds `<shop-type>-primer.md` to its content
  set.
- `.claude/shop/` — **shop-managed**. Files here are written once at
  bootstrap and then owned by the shop. `update` never touches anything
  under `.claude/shop/`.
- `CLAUDE.md` at the target root — **canonical-managed** (this is what
  changes under F). Its content is a deterministic function of
  `.claude/shop/type.md` and canonical package data.

Note on `.claude/agents/`: today the agent role-prompt files live there
and are managed by `update`. Under F they remain where they are;
`.claude/agents/` is canonical-managed in the same sense as
`.claude/canonical/`. The PDR does not propose renaming `.claude/agents/`
into `.claude/canonical/agents/`; that is a follow-up consolidation if
desired.

Rationale for path choice: ownership is legible from the path alone.
`.claude/canonical/...` says "do not hand-edit"; `.claude/shop/...` says
"this is yours". A new operator does not need to read a contract
document to know which is which.

### Migration

A legacy shop (one bootstrapped under the pre-F contract) has a
monolithic `CLAUDE.md` containing canonical primer text inlined,
shop-authored prose interleaved, and no `.claude/shop/` directory.
This product's own lead shop is exactly such a legacy shop today, so
the migration UX is concrete, not hypothetical.

The recommended migration shape is **refuse-with-diagnostic** (not
auto-migrate). When `update` runs in a target that lacks
`.claude/shop/type.md`, it:

- Exits non-zero.
- Writes a diagnostic to stderr naming the migration steps the
  operator must perform: create `.claude/shop/{name,type,primer}.md`
  with appropriate contents; move any shop-authored prose from the
  existing `CLAUDE.md` into `.claude/shop/primer.md`; then re-run
  `update`.
- Touches no files in the target.

Rationale: auto-migrate would have to parse a free-form `CLAUDE.md` to
separate canonical text from shop-authored prose, with no reliable
delimiter. That's exactly the brittleness alternative D was rejected
for. Refuse-with-diagnostic keeps the migration step explicit, manual,
and reviewable; the cost is a one-time per-shop operator action.

A future `shop-templates migrate` subcommand could automate the file
moves once we accept the legacy `CLAUDE.md` will need a sentinel or
operator-marked region to make the split deterministic. That is out of
scope for this PDR.

## Scenarios this PDR contradicts

- **39** (`update-preserves-shop-owned-claude-md-edits`). Under F,
  `update` overwrites `CLAUDE.md` from canonical. The original
  invariant ("byte-for-byte preservation of `CLAUDE.md`") no longer
  holds against `CLAUDE.md` itself. The replacement invariant is that
  `update` preserves shop-authored content **in
  `.claude/shop/primer.md`** byte-for-byte; `CLAUDE.md` itself is
  canonical-managed and outside the preservation contract. The PO
  proposes **amending** scenario 39 (not superseding) — the same
  identifier should continue to express "update does not destroy the
  shop's authored content," with the storage location updated to
  `.claude/shop/primer.md`. Architect's call whether amend or supersede.

## Scenarios this PDR preserves (unchanged)

- **31** (bootstrap writes top-level `CLAUDE.md`) — preserved.
  `bootstrap` continues to write `CLAUDE.md`; the file is non-empty
  (its body contains the four import lines plus a short header).
- **44** (canonical primer is package data) — preserved. The canonical
  primer continues to ship as package data, now as dedicated
  `<shop-type>-primer.md` files. A second canonical asset
  (`<shop-type>-claude-md.md` or similar — the canonical `CLAUDE.md`
  template body) is added; this is additive to 44, not a contradiction.
- **56** (`update-rejects-missing-target-with-argparse-error`) — the
  error-path clause that `CLAUDE.md` is unchanged from before the
  invocation **holds**, because `update` writes nothing on argparse
  error. The wording can stand verbatim; on the success path,
  `CLAUDE.md` may now change, but the error-path invariant is
  unrelated to the success-path contract.
- **40, 41** (`.gitignore` and `.beads/` non-touch invariants) —
  unaffected; F changes only the `CLAUDE.md` surface.

## Scenarios this PDR amends (Architect call)

- **45** (`bootstrap-generated-claude-md-names-the-shops-identity`).
  Under F, the substring check `that file contains the literal substring
  "<shop_name>"` no longer passes against `CLAUDE.md` directly — the
  shop name lives in `.claude/shop/name.md`, imported by `CLAUDE.md`.
  Two phrasings, Architect's pick:
  - **Phrasing A (PO leans):** retain the assertion at the level of
    the assistant-resolved `CLAUDE.md` (imports included). The
    scenario pins "the shop's identity is named in the agent's
    startup context," which is what we actually care about.
  - **Phrasing B:** amend the scenario to assert "the `CLAUDE.md`
    import graph names the shop identity" — i.e., `CLAUDE.md` imports
    `.claude/shop/name.md`, and that file contains `<shop_name>`.
    More mechanism-precise; couples the scenario to the import
    discipline.
  PO leans toward Phrasing A.
- **71 / 72 / 73** (Monitor-arming directive in canonical and
  bootstrap-generated `CLAUDE.md`). Same dichotomy as in v2 of this
  PDR. Under F, the directive lives in
  `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md`, reachable from
  `CLAUDE.md` via import. Phrasing A (assertion at the level of
  assistant-observable behavior — the directive is present in the
  agent's startup context) is preserved as-is. Phrasing B (assert the
  storage location directly) requires amendment to name the canonical
  primer file. PO leans Phrasing A — the scenarios pin agent-
  observable behavior, not storage location.

## New scenarios this PDR will author after acceptance

1. **Canonical `CLAUDE.md` body ships as package data, per shop type.**
   `shop-templates` exposes a canonical `CLAUDE.md` template body for
   each shop type via the same package-data surface as the primer and
   role-prompt templates. (Companion to scenario 44.)
2. **Bootstrap writes the canonical-managed `CLAUDE.md`** whose body
   is the canonical template body for the shop's type, byte-for-byte.
3. **Bootstrap writes `.claude/shop/name.md`** containing exactly the
   value passed as `--shop-name`, with no other content.
4. **Bootstrap writes `.claude/shop/type.md`** containing exactly the
   value passed as `--shop-type` (`bc` or `lead`), with no other content.
5. **Bootstrap writes `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md`** with
   the canonical primer for the chosen shop type, byte-for-byte from
   package data.
6. **Bootstrap writes `.claude/shop/primer.md`** as a shop-authored
   placeholder (empty or near-empty); the operator may populate it
   later. (Companion to "shop-authored prose has a dedicated home"
   under F.)
7. **`CLAUDE.md` body imports the four typed files in a fixed order**
   — property-level assertion: when the assistant resolves `CLAUDE.md`,
   the contents of all four files are present in its startup context.
   Literal import syntax is an Implementer follow-up against
   authoritative Claude Code documentation.
8. **`update` overwrites `CLAUDE.md`** with the canonical `CLAUDE.md`
   body for the shop's declared type when the target's `CLAUDE.md`
   has drifted from canonical.
9. **`update` overwrites `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md`**
   when the canonical primer has drifted from the target's copy.
10. **`update` does not touch `.claude/shop/name.md`** under any
    circumstances on the success path.
11. **`update` does not touch `.claude/shop/type.md`** under any
    circumstances on the success path.
12. **`update` does not touch `.claude/shop/primer.md`** under any
    circumstances on the success path (this is the F-shaped
    replacement for the old scenario 39's intent — shop-authored
    content is preserved, just in a different file).
13. **`update` is idempotent** when both canonical-managed files
    already match package data byte-for-byte — exit zero, no
    diagnostic, no file mtime change.
14. **`update` reads `.claude/shop/type.md`** to determine which
    shop type's canonical templates to apply. (Pins the data flow:
    the shop's declared type at bootstrap is authoritative for
    subsequent updates, not a flag re-passed at update time.)
15. **Migration scenario — refuse-with-diagnostic.** When `update`
    runs against a target that lacks `.claude/shop/type.md` (legacy
    bootstrap), `update` exits non-zero, writes a diagnostic naming
    the migration steps the operator must perform, and touches no
    files in the target.

## Open questions (for Architect)

1. **Phrasing A vs. Phrasing B** for scenarios 45 and 71/72/73 — the
   Architect's call when assigning the new scenario set.
2. **Whether to amend or supersede scenario 39.** PO recommends amend
   (same identifier, new storage location for the preservation
   contract).
3. **Exact filenames** for the canonical assets under
   `shop-templates`' package data — the PDR names `<shop-type>-
   primer.md` and a parallel canonical `CLAUDE.md` body asset, but
   does not pin the literal filename of the latter (e.g.,
   `<shop-type>-claude-md.md` vs. `claude-md-<shop-type>.md`). An
   Implementer concern.
4. **Whether `update`'s migration exit code should be 1 or another
   non-zero value**, and whether the diagnostic should be
   machine-parseable (e.g., a stable error tag). PO recommends 1 with
   a free-form but consistent diagnostic; Architect may refine.
5. **Whether `.claude/shop/type.md` and `.claude/shop/name.md` should
   be plain-text single-line files** or a single combined YAML/TOML
   file. PO recommends two plain-text files for path-level legibility
   (the path itself names what it holds); Architect may prefer one
   structured file for forward extensibility (e.g., adding a future
   `shop-version` field).
```

# Message-type discriminator question

The two message types in play want different vehicles per the lead-shop
template:
- New capability (no scenario pins it yet) → `assign_scenarios`
- Capability exists but pinned the wrong way → `request_bugfix`

You can:
- **Bundle as one `assign_scenarios`** — author the 15 new scenarios; the
  amendments to 39 etc are scenario register reconciliation the BC
  performs as a side effect when implementing the new scenarios.
- **Split into two dispatches** — `assign_scenarios` for the 15;
  `request_bugfix` for the 39 amendment specifically. Risk: ordering
  matters and the BC could implement them inconsistently.
- **Bundle as `request_bugfix`** — frame the whole thing as fixing the
  existing CLAUDE.md propagation contract, with the new scenarios as the
  surface area of the fix. Sense-check this: 15 net-new scenarios is a
  lot of mass for a bugfix vehicle.

Make the call. Justify it in the dispatch description.

# Operational details

- `shop-msg` is at `.venv/bin/shop-msg`. Use absolute path.
- Run `shop-msg pending outbox --lead-root .` first to see if there's
  any in-flight state with the templates BC that should inform
  ordering (e.g., an open dispatch you'd be racing against).
- Sibling BC clone is at `repos/shopsystem-templates/`. You may inspect
  it read-only to verify pre-state; do NOT edit it.
- PDR-003 is at `pdr/003-claude-md-update-propagation.md`. Read it
  before dispatching — the dispatch should reference the PDR by path.
- Scenarios you'll dispatch should be authored as `.gherkin` files
  under `features/` first (the PDR did not pre-author them), or you
  can dispatch with the scenarios specified in the message body and
  let the BC author them — check the canonical lead-shop / shop-msg
  conventions to determine which path this product uses. (Recent
  commits like `f0c9f61 features: scenarios 71-76 — brief 003
  realization revision (lead-o00)` suggest the lead shop authors
  scenarios in `features/` before dispatch.)

# Form of your output

Report under 600 words:
- One paragraph on pre-state verification, with the concrete demonstration
  you cited.
- Your call on 39 (amend vs supersede) and on Phrasing A/B for 45/71-73,
  with one-sentence justification each.

- Anything that turned out to need a clarify from the BC before dispatch

Then close `lead-eyx`.

```text
1	# PDR-003 — CLAUDE.md update propagation
2	
3	**Status:** Proposed
4	**Date:** 2026-05-14
5	**Author:** lead-po (subagent dispatch)
6	**Beads:** lead-5kn
7	
8	## Context
9	
10	A shop must bootstrap and run self-contained — the `repos/` directory of
11	the lead-shop product is not assumed available inside a bootstrapped shop.
12	That was the bootstrap-time framing. The **temporal corollary** has not
13	been pinned by scenarios: once bootstrapped, a shop must stay self-
14	contained as the canonical evolves. Concretely, when the canonical
15	`CLAUDE.md` primer in `shopsystem-templates` changes (e.g., the Monitor-
16	arming directive added by scenarios 71/72/73), bootstrapped shops must
17	receive that change through `shop-templates update`, not through manual
18	re-editing in each shop.
19	
20	The current contract pins the **opposite**: `shop-templates update` is
21	explicitly forbidden to touch the target's `CLAUDE.md`, even when the
22	canonical primer has changed since bootstrap (scenario 39, reinforced by
23	scenario 56's error-path invariant). The source comment at
24	`repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/cli.py:585-587` cites four
25	hashes as the "non-touch invariant on CLAUDE.md / .gitignore / .beads/".
26	
27	The deeper tension is that `CLAUDE.md` is **part-canonical, part-per-
28	shop**: scenario 45 requires the bootstrap-generated `CLAUDE.md` to name
29	the shop's own identity (shop name, role set), while scenario 44 holds
30	the canonical primer as package-data source-of-truth. Empirical evidence:
31	this product's `repos/shopsystem-templates/CLAUDE.md` carries canonical
32	primer text *and* shop-authored sections like "Repo layout" and "Build &
33	Test" that no canonical template would dictate. Any propagation contract
34	must reconcile these.
35	
36	## Design alternatives
37	
38	### A. Full replace
39	`update` overwrites `CLAUDE.md` from canonical unconditionally. Cleanest
40	mechanism; loses scenario 45 (no shop identity in the result) and erases
41	shop-authored prose. Rejected.
42	
43	### B. Identity parameterization
44	`CLAUDE.md` becomes fully canonical once shop name and role set are
45	template parameters resolved at update time. The shop owns *no*
46	`CLAUDE.md` content. Pure mechanism, but requires that no shop ever
47	benefits from local primer additions — empirically false for the
48	`shopsystem-templates` BC repo today, which carries shop-authored "Repo
49	layout" and "Build & Test" sections. Would force migration of all
50	shop-authored prose elsewhere before adoption. Rejected as the first
51	move; viable as a longer-term direction once the canonical primer is
52	parameterized.
53	
54	### C. Sentinel-delimited canonical region
55	The canonical primer template defines a region delimited by sentinel
56	comments (e.g., `<!-- shop-templates:canonical:begin -->` and
57	`<!-- shop-templates:canonical:end -->`). `update` replaces only the
58	text between sentinels in the target's `CLAUDE.md`; everything outside
59	the sentinels is preserved byte-for-byte. Requires mutating `CLAUDE.md`
60	on every update, which contradicts scenario 39's byte-for-byte non-touch
61	invariant and forces amending scenarios 39 and 56 to scope the invariant
62	to outside-sentinel content. Rejected in favor of F (below); F achieves
63	the same propagation outcome with cleaner ownership and no merge logic.
64	
65	### D. Sectioned merge by header name
66	Like C but keyed on Markdown headers instead of sentinels. Rejected as
67	fragile: header text edits in either the canonical template or the
68	target would silently break the merge contract. Sentinels are explicit;
69	headers are not.
70	
71	### E. Canonical primer in dedicated imported files, with shop-owned `CLAUDE.md`
72	The canonical primer content lives in a dedicated file under
73	`.claude/canonical/`. `CLAUDE.md` is shop-owned end to end and imports
74	the canonical file via Claude Code's `@<filename>` directive. `update`
75	overwrites the canonical imported file in place and never touches
76	`CLAUDE.md`. Preserves scenarios 39, 45, 56 verbatim. Rejected per
77	stakeholder directive (2026-05-14) in favor of F, which trades some
78	scenario preservation for a cleaner ownership story.
79	
80	### F. Canonical-managed `CLAUDE.md` with four typed imports (RECOMMENDED)
81	`CLAUDE.md` is **canonical-managed and overwritten by `update`**. Its
82	body is intentionally minimal: a short header plus a fixed sequence of
83	`@<filename>` imports. The substantive content lives in four typed
84	files, each with a single owner:
85	
86	1. **Shop-name file** — contains the shop's own name (the literal string
87	   passed as `--shop-name` at bootstrap). **Bootstrap-only**, never
88	   touched by `update`. Owner: the shop.
89	2. **Shop-type file** — contains the shop's type token (`bc` or `lead`).
90	   **Bootstrap-only**, never touched by `update`. Owner: the shop.
91	3. **Canonical primer file** — the canonical router-prompt content for
92	   this shop type. Ships as package data; **overwritten by `update`** on
93	   drift. Owner: canonical (`shop-templates` package data).
94	4. **Shop-specific primer file** — shop-authored prose (e.g., "Repo
95	   layout", "Build & Test", anything the shop wants to add). Created
96	   at bootstrap as an empty (or near-empty) placeholder. **Never touched
97	   by `update`**. Owner: the shop.
98	
99	`CLAUDE.md` itself is a deterministic function of canonical package data
100	and the shop type — the same `CLAUDE.md` body is written into every shop
101	of a given type. Bootstrap parameters (`--shop-name`, `--shop-type`) are
102	captured in the two shop-owned dotfiles imported by `CLAUDE.md`, not
103	spliced into `CLAUDE.md` itself.
104	
105	Pros:
106	- **Cleanest ownership story.** Every file has exactly one owner. The
107	  path itself signals ownership (see "File-path convention" below). A
108	  reader can answer "who writes this file?" without reading its
109	  contents.
110	- **Canonical content propagation is unconditional and bulletproof.**
111	  The shop cannot accidentally remove an import or hand-edit `CLAUDE.md`
112	  in a way that loses canonical content, because `update` rewrites
113	  `CLAUDE.md` from canonical every time. No drift is possible.
114	- **No merge logic, no sentinels, no parsing.** `update` performs four
115	  operations at most: rewrite `CLAUDE.md`, rewrite the canonical primer
116	  file, leave the two shop-owned files alone, leave the shop-specific
117	  primer alone.
118	- **Shop-authored prose has a dedicated home.** The shop-specific
119	  primer file is the named, expected place for "Repo layout", "Build &
120	  Test", and other shop-local content. Separation of concerns is
121	  explicit at the file boundary, not buried inside a multi-author file.
122	- **Symmetric with how `update` already handles `.claude/agents/`.** Both
123	  surfaces become "canonical package data poured in place"; `CLAUDE.md`
124	  joins the same model rather than being a special case.
125	
126	Cons:
127	- **Scenario 39 is contradicted.** `update` does overwrite `CLAUDE.md`.
128	  Operators who hand-edited `CLAUDE.md` under the old contract lose
129	  those edits on `update`. A migration story is required (see below).
130	  The recommendation accepts this hit deliberately; the cleaner
131	  ownership story is judged to be worth the cost.
132	- **Four files instead of one.** More bootstrap surface; a new reader
133	  has more files to orient to. Mitigated by `CLAUDE.md` being short and
134	  literally naming each import (e.g., `@.claude/shop/name.md  # the
135	  shop's own name`) so the import graph is self-documenting.
136	- **Depends on Claude Code's `@<filename>` import mechanism** being
137	  stable across consumers (CLI, IDE extension, future agent runtimes).
138	  Same dependency as alternative E.
139	- **Bootstrap parameter capture lives in a file, not in prose.** A
140	  human glancing at `CLAUDE.md` will see only the imports, not the
141	  shop's name directly. They must follow `@.claude/shop/name.md` to
142	  see "shopsystem-product". Mitigated by `CLAUDE.md` placing the
143	  shop-identity imports at the top, so the indirection is visible
144	  immediately.
145	
146	## Recommendation
147	
148	Adopt **alternative F — canonical-managed `CLAUDE.md` with four typed
149	imports**. The contract becomes:
150	
151	1. Canonical primer content for each shop type ships as a dedicated file
152	   in `shop-templates` package data (`lead-primer.md`, `bc-primer.md`),
153	   parallel to the role-prompt template files already shipped there. A
154	   canonical `CLAUDE.md` template body also ships as package data, one
155	   per shop type.
156	2. Bootstrap writes **five** files into the target shop:
157	   - `CLAUDE.md` at the target root (canonical-managed).
158	   - `.claude/shop/name.md` (shop-owned, bootstrap-only) — the literal
159	     value passed as `--shop-name`.
160	   - `.claude/shop/type.md` (shop-owned, bootstrap-only) — the literal
161	     value passed as `--shop-type`.
162	   - `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md` (canonical-managed) —
163	     the canonical router-prompt body.
164	   - `.claude/shop/primer.md` (shop-owned, bootstrap-only placeholder)
165	     — empty or near-empty; the shop fills it in over time.
166	   `CLAUDE.md`'s body imports those four files in a fixed order with
167	   short headings that name each import's role.
168	3. `update` overwrites two files: `CLAUDE.md` (from the canonical
169	   `CLAUDE.md` template for the shop's declared type) and
170	   `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md` (from the canonical primer
171	   for the shop's declared type). It reads `.claude/shop/type.md` to
172	   know which shop type's canonical to apply.
173	4. `update` is idempotent when both canonical-managed files already
174	   match package data byte-for-byte — no diagnostic, no file mtime
175	   change, exit zero.
176	5. `update` does **not** touch `.claude/shop/name.md`,
177	   `.claude/shop/type.md`, or `.claude/shop/primer.md` under any
178	   circumstances.
179	6. **Migration** for legacy shops (including this product's own lead
180	   shop today, whose `CLAUDE.md` predates the four-file split): see the
181	   "Migration" subsection below.
182	
183	### File-path convention
184	
185	The split lives under `.claude/`:
186	
187	- `.claude/canonical/` — **canonical-managed**. Files here are written
188	  by `bootstrap` and rewritten by `update` from package data. Operators
189	  must not hand-edit; edits will be silently reverted on the next
190	  `update`. Today this directory already houses the canonical agent
191	  files (`bc-implementer.md`, etc., via `.claude/agents/` — see note
192	  below); alternative F adds `<shop-type>-primer.md` to its content
193	  set.
194	- `.claude/shop/` — **shop-managed**. Files here are written once at
195	  bootstrap and then owned by the shop. `update` never touches anything
196	  under `.claude/shop/`.
197	- `CLAUDE.md` at the target root — **canonical-managed** (this is what
198	  changes under F). Its content is a deterministic function of
199	  `.claude/shop/type.md` and canonical package data.
200	
201	Note on `.claude/agents/`: today the agent role-prompt files live there
202	and are managed by `update`. Under F they remain where they are;
203	`.claude/agents/` is canonical-managed in the same sense as
204	`.claude/canonical/`. The PDR does not propose renaming `.claude/agents/`
205	into `.claude/canonical/agents/`; that is a follow-up consolidation if
206	desired.
207	
208	Rationale for path choice: ownership is legible from the path alone.
209	`.claude/canonical/...` says "do not hand-edit"; `.claude/shop/...` says
210	"this is yours". A new operator does not need to read a contract
211	document to know which is which.
212	
213	### Migration
214	
215	A legacy shop (one bootstrapped under the pre-F contract) has a
216	monolithic `CLAUDE.md` containing canonical primer text inlined,
217	shop-authored prose interleaved, and no `.claude/shop/` directory.
218	This product's own lead shop is exactly such a legacy shop today, so
219	the migration UX is concrete, not hypothetical.
220	
221	The recommended migration shape is **refuse-with-diagnostic** (not
222	auto-migrate). When `update` runs in a target that lacks
223	`.claude/shop/type.md`, it:
224	
225	- Exits non-zero.
226	- Writes a diagnostic to stderr naming the migration steps the
227	  operator must perform: create `.claude/shop/{name,type,primer}.md`
228	  with appropriate contents; move any shop-authored prose from the
229	  existing `CLAUDE.md` into `.claude/shop/primer.md`; then re-run
230	  `update`.
231	- Touches no files in the target.
232	
233	Rationale: auto-migrate would have to parse a free-form `CLAUDE.md` to
234	separate canonical text from shop-authored prose, with no reliable
235	delimiter. That's exactly the brittleness alternative D was rejected
236	for. Refuse-with-diagnostic keeps the migration step explicit, manual,
237	and reviewable; the cost is a one-time per-shop operator action.
238	
239	A future `shop-templates migrate` subcommand could automate the file
240	moves once we accept the legacy `CLAUDE.md` will need a sentinel or
241	operator-marked region to make the split deterministic. That is out of
242	scope for this PDR.
243	
244	## Scenarios this PDR contradicts
245	
246	- **39** (`update-preserves-shop-owned-claude-md-edits`). Under F,
247	  `update` overwrites `CLAUDE.md` from canonical. The original
248	  invariant ("byte-for-byte preservation of `CLAUDE.md`") no longer
249	  holds against `CLAUDE.md` itself. The replacement invariant is that
250	  `update` preserves shop-authored content **in
251	  `.claude/shop/primer.md`** byte-for-byte; `CLAUDE.md` itself is
252	  canonical-managed and outside the preservation contract. The PO
253	  proposes **amending** scenario 39 (not superseding) — the same
254	  identifier should continue to express "update does not destroy the
255	  shop's authored content," with the storage location updated to
256	  `.claude/shop/primer.md`. Architect's call whether amend or supersede.
257	
258	## Scenarios this PDR preserves (unchanged)
259	
260	- **31** (bootstrap writes top-level `CLAUDE.md`) — preserved.
261	  `bootstrap` continues to write `CLAUDE.md`; the file is non-empty
262	  (its body contains the four import lines plus a short header).
263	- **44** (canonical primer is package data) — preserved. The canonical
264	  primer continues to ship as package data, now as dedicated
265	  `<shop-type>-primer.md` files. A second canonical asset
266	  (`<shop-type>-claude-md.md` or similar — the canonical `CLAUDE.md`
267	  template body) is added; this is additive to 44, not a contradiction.
268	- **56** (`update-rejects-missing-target-with-argparse-error`) — the
269	  error-path clause that `CLAUDE.md` is unchanged from before the
270	  invocation **holds**, because `update` writes nothing on argparse
271	  error. The wording can stand verbatim; on the success path,
272	  `CLAUDE.md` may now change, but the error-path invariant is
273	  unrelated to the success-path contract.
274	- **40, 41** (`.gitignore` and `.beads/` non-touch invariants) —
275	  unaffected; F changes only the `CLAUDE.md` surface.
276	
277	## Scenarios this PDR amends (Architect call)
278	
279	- **45** (`bootstrap-generated-claude-md-names-the-shops-identity`).
280	  Under F, the substring check `that file contains the literal substring
281	  "<shop_name>"` no longer passes against `CLAUDE.md` directly — the
282	  shop name lives in `.claude/shop/name.md`, imported by `CLAUDE.md`.
283	  Two phrasings, Architect's pick:
284	  - **Phrasing A (PO leans):** retain the assertion at the level of
285	    the assistant-resolved `CLAUDE.md` (imports included). The
286	    scenario pins "the shop's identity is named in the agent's
287	    startup context," which is what we actually care about.
288	  - **Phrasing B:** amend the scenario to assert "the `CLAUDE.md`
289	    import graph names the shop identity" — i.e., `CLAUDE.md` imports
290	    `.claude/shop/name.md`, and that file contains `<shop_name>`.
291	    More mechanism-precise; couples the scenario to the import
292	    discipline.
293	  PO leans toward Phrasing A.
294	- **71 / 72 / 73** (Monitor-arming directive in canonical and
295	  bootstrap-generated `CLAUDE.md`). Same dichotomy as in v2 of this
296	  PDR. Under F, the directive lives in
297	  `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md`, reachable from
298	  `CLAUDE.md` via import. Phrasing A (assertion at the level of
299	  assistant-observable behavior — the directive is present in the
300	  agent's startup context) is preserved as-is. Phrasing B (assert the
301	  storage location directly) requires amendment to name the canonical
302	  primer file. PO leans Phrasing A — the scenarios pin agent-
303	  observable behavior, not storage location.
304	
305	## New scenarios this PDR will author after acceptance
306	
307	1. **Canonical `CLAUDE.md` body ships as package data, per shop type.**
308	   `shop-templates` exposes a canonical `CLAUDE.md` template body for
309	   each shop type via the same package-data surface as the primer and
310	   role-prompt templates. (Companion to scenario 44.)
311	2. **Bootstrap writes the canonical-managed `CLAUDE.md`** whose body
312	   is the canonical template body for the shop's type, byte-for-byte.
313	3. **Bootstrap writes `.claude/shop/name.md`** containing exactly the
314	   value passed as `--shop-name`, with no other content.
315	4. **Bootstrap writes `.claude/shop/type.md`** containing exactly the
316	   value passed as `--shop-type` (`bc` or `lead`), with no other content.
317	5. **Bootstrap writes `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md`** with
318	   the canonical primer for the chosen shop type, byte-for-byte from
319	   package data.
320	6. **Bootstrap writes `.claude/shop/primer.md`** as a shop-authored
321	   placeholder (empty or near-empty); the operator may populate it
322	   later. (Companion to "shop-authored prose has a dedicated home"
323	   under F.)
324	7. **`CLAUDE.md` body imports the four typed files in a fixed order**
325	   — property-level assertion: when the assistant resolves `CLAUDE.md`,
326	   the contents of all four files are present in its startup context.
327	   Literal import syntax is an Implementer follow-up against
328	   authoritative Claude Code documentation.
329	8. **`update` overwrites `CLAUDE.md`** with the canonical `CLAUDE.md`
330	   body for the shop's declared type when the target's `CLAUDE.md`
331	   has drifted from canonical.
332	9. **`update` overwrites `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md`**
333	   when the canonical primer has drifted from the target's copy.
334	10. **`update` does not touch `.claude/shop/name.md`** under any
335	    circumstances on the success path.
336	11. **`update` does not touch `.claude/shop/type.md`** under any
337	    circumstances on the success path.
338	12. **`update` does not touch `.claude/shop/primer.md`** under any
339	    circumstances on the success path (this is the F-shaped
340	    replacement for the old scenario 39's intent — shop-authored
341	    content is preserved, just in a different file).
342	13. **`update` is idempotent** when both canonical-managed files
343	    already match package data byte-for-byte — exit zero, no
344	    diagnostic, no file mtime change.
345	14. **`update` reads `.claude/shop/type.md`** to determine which
346	    shop type's canonical templates to apply. (Pins the data flow:
347	    the shop's declared type at bootstrap is authoritative for
348	    subsequent updates, not a flag re-passed at update time.)
349	15. **Migration scenario — refuse-with-diagnostic.** When `update`
350	    runs against a target that lacks `.claude/shop/type.md` (legacy
351	    bootstrap), `update` exits non-zero, writes a diagnostic naming
352	    the migration steps the operator must perform, and touches no
353	    files in the target.
354	
355	## Open questions (for Architect)
356	
357	1. **Phrasing A vs. Phrasing B** for scenarios 45 and 71/72/73 — the
358	   Architect's call when assigning the new scenario set.
359	2. **Whether to amend or supersede scenario 39.** PO recommends amend
360	   (same identifier, new storage location for the preservation
361	   contract).
362	3. **Exact filenames** for the canonical assets under
363	   `shop-templates`' package data — the PDR names `<shop-type>-
364	   primer.md` and a parallel canonical `CLAUDE.md` body asset, but
365	   does not pin the literal filename of the latter (e.g.,
366	   `<shop-type>-claude-md.md` vs. `claude-md-<shop-type>.md`). An
367	   Implementer concern.
368	4. **Whether `update`'s migration exit code should be 1 or another
369	   non-zero value**, and whether the diagnostic should be
370	   machine-parseable (e.g., a stable error tag). PO recommends 1 with
371	   a free-form but consistent diagnostic; Architect may refine.
372	5. **Whether `.claude/shop/type.md` and `.claude/shop/name.md` should
373	   be plain-text single-line files** or a single combined YAML/TOML
374	   file. PO recommends two plain-text files for path-level legibility
375	   (the path itself names what it holds); Architect may prefer one
376	   structured file for forward extensibility (e.g., adding a future
377	   `shop-version` field).
378
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
5	  Scenario 58 pinned a "SessionStart" hook in ".claude/settings.json" carrying the inotifywait pipeline. Test-harness BC empirically demonstrated a hook-model mismatch: Claude Code awaits SessionStart hooks synchronously, so a foreground "inotifywait -m" never returns and session startup hangs / times out. The schema and the host prereqs are fine; the structural mismatch is that synchronous hook delivery is incompatible with a long-running watcher process. Brief 003 (lead-0h1) commits the invariant that every shop is reactive on session start — the realization revision (lead-o00) shifts the mechanism: instead of the SessionStart hook running the watcher, the canonical "CLAUDE.md" primer instructs the router (Claude session) to arm the in-session "Monitor" tool on the validated pipeline at session start. Phrasing A is verifiable today by reading bootstrap-output `CLAUDE.md` directly. Under alt F, scenario 71 asks "does the canonical primer template body contain Monitor?" — that body is now `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md`, which is the canonical primer template surface. Phrasing A holds for 71/72 verbatim because they probe "the canonical primer template" via the package-data surface, and that surface continues to exist under F — just at a renamed asset.
```

Scenario 73 asserts `CLAUDE.md`'s content directly: `content of that file contains "Monitor"`. Under alt F, the bootstrap-written `CLAUDE.md` body is a minimal header + four `@`-imports — it does NOT contain "Monitor" literally; that lives one indirection away in `.claude/canonical/lead-primer.md`. - **Phrasing B for 73 only.** Scenario 73 asserts substrings against the *bootstrap-written `CLAUDE.md` file directly*. Phrasing A is empirically false post-F. Amend 73 to assert that `CLAUDE.md` imports `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md`, and that file contains the substrings.  `./pdr/003-claude-md-update-propagation.md`

```text
1	# PDR-003 — CLAUDE.md update propagation
2	
3	**Status:** Proposed
4	**Date:** 2026-05-14
5	**Author:** lead-po (subagent dispatch)
6	**Beads:** lead-5kn
7	
8	## Context
9	
10	A shop must bootstrap and run self-contained — the `repos/` directory of
11	the lead-shop product is not assumed available inside a bootstrapped shop.
12	That was the bootstrap-time framing. The **temporal corollary** has not
13	been pinned by scenarios: once bootstrapped, a shop must stay self-
14	contained as the canonical evolves. Concretely, when the canonical
15	`CLAUDE.md` primer in `shopsystem-templates` changes (e.g., the Monitor-
16	arming directive added by scenarios 71/72/73), bootstrapped shops must
17	receive that change through `shop-templates update`, not through manual
18	re-editing in each shop.
19	
20	The current contract pins the **opposite**: `shop-templates update` is
21	explicitly forbidden to touch the target's `CLAUDE.md`, even when the
22	canonical primer has changed since bootstrap (scenario 39, reinforced by
23	scenario 56's error-path invariant). The source comment at
24	`repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/cli.py:585-587` cites four
25	hashes as the "non-touch invariant on CLAUDE.md / .gitignore / .beads/".
26	
27	The deeper tension is that `CLAUDE.md` is **part-canonical, part-per-
28	shop**: scenario 45 requires the bootstrap-generated `CLAUDE.md` to name
29	the shop's own identity (shop name, role set), while scenario 44 holds
30	the canonical primer as package-data source-of-truth. Empirical evidence:
31	this product's `repos/shopsystem-templates/CLAUDE.md` carries canonical
32	primer text *and* shop-authored sections like "Repo layout" and "Build &
33	Test" that no canonical template would dictate. Any propagation contract
34	must reconcile these.
35	
36	## Design alternatives
37	
38	### A. Full replace
39	`update` overwrites `CLAUDE.md` from canonical unconditionally. Cleanest
40	mechanism; loses scenario 45 (no shop identity in the result) and erases
41	shop-authored prose. Rejected.
42	
43	### B. Identity parameterization
44	`CLAUDE.md` becomes fully canonical once shop name and role set are
45	template parameters resolved at update time. The shop owns *no*
46	`CLAUDE.md` content. Pure mechanism, but requires that no shop ever
47	benefits from local primer additions — empirically false for the
48	`shopsystem-templates` BC repo today, which carries shop-authored "Repo
49	layout" and "Build & Test" sections. Would force migration of all
50	shop-authored prose elsewhere before adoption. Rejected as the first
51	move; viable as a longer-term direction once the canonical primer is
52	parameterized.
53	
54	### C. Sentinel-delimited canonical region
55	The canonical primer template defines a region delimited by sentinel
56	comments (e.g., `<!-- shop-templates:canonical:begin -->` and
57	`<!-- shop-templates:canonical:end -->`). `update` replaces only the
58	text between sentinels in the target's `CLAUDE.md`; everything outside
59	the sentinels is preserved byte-for-byte. Requires mutating `CLAUDE.md`
60	on every update, which contradicts scenario 39's byte-for-byte non-touch
61	invariant and forces amending scenarios 39 and 56 to scope the invariant
62	to outside-sentinel content. Rejected in favor of F (below); F achieves
63	the same propagation outcome with cleaner ownership and no merge logic.
64	
65	### D. Sectioned merge by header name
66	Like C but keyed on Markdown headers instead of sentinels. Rejected as
67	fragile: header text edits in either the canonical template or the
68	target would silently break the merge contract. Sentinels are explicit;
69	headers are not.
70	
71	### E. Canonical primer in dedicated imported files, with shop-owned `CLAUDE.md`
72	The canonical primer content lives in a dedicated file under
73	`.claude/canonical/`. `CLAUDE.md` is shop-owned end to end and imports
74	the canonical file via Claude Code's `@<filename>` directive. `update`
75	overwrites the canonical imported file in place and never touches
76	`CLAUDE.md`. Preserves scenarios 39, 45, 56 verbatim. Rejected per
77	stakeholder directive (2026-05-14) in favor of F, which trades some
78	scenario preservation for a cleaner ownership story.
79	
80	### F. Canonical-managed `CLAUDE.md` with four typed imports (RECOMMENDED)
81	`CLAUDE.md` is **canonical-managed and overwritten by `update`**. Its
82	body is intentionally minimal: a short header plus a fixed sequence of
83	`@<filename>` imports. The substantive content lives in four typed
84	files, each with a single owner:
85	
86	1. **Shop-name file** — contains the shop's own name (the literal string
87	   passed as `--shop-name` at bootstrap). **Bootstrap-only**, never
88	   touched by `update`. Owner: the shop.
89	2. **Shop-type file** — contains the shop's type token (`bc` or `lead`).
90	   **Bootstrap-only**, never touched by `update`. Owner: the shop.
91	3. **Canonical primer file** — the canonical router-prompt content for
92	   this shop type. Ships as package data; **overwritten by `update`** on
93	   drift. Owner: canonical (`shop-templates` package data).
94	4. **Shop-specific primer file** — shop-authored prose (e.g., "Repo
95	   layout", "Build & Test", anything the shop wants to add). Created
96	   at bootstrap as an empty (or near-empty) placeholder. **Never touched
97	   by `update`**. Owner: the shop.
98	
99	`CLAUDE.md` itself is a deterministic function of canonical package data
100	and the shop type — the same `CLAUDE.md` body is written into every shop
101	of a given type. Bootstrap parameters (`--shop-name`, `--shop-type`) are
102	captured in the two shop-owned dotfiles imported by `CLAUDE.md`, not
103	spliced into `CLAUDE.md` itself.
104	
105	Pros:
106	- **Cleanest ownership story.** Every file has exactly one owner. The
107	  path itself signals ownership (see "File-path convention" below). A
108	  reader can answer "who writes this file?" without reading its
109	  contents.
110	- **Canonical content propagation is unconditional and bulletproof.**
111	  The shop cannot accidentally remove an import or hand-edit `CLAUDE.md`
112	  in a way that loses canonical content, because `update` rewrites
113	  `CLAUDE.md` from canonical every time. No drift is possible.
114	- **No merge logic, no sentinels, no parsing.** `update` performs four
115	  operations at most: rewrite `CLAUDE.md`, rewrite the canonical primer
116	  file, leave the two shop-owned files alone, leave the shop-specific
117	  primer alone.
118	- **Shop-authored prose has a dedicated home.** The shop-specific
119	  primer file is the named, expected place for "Repo layout", "Build &
120	  Test", and other shop-local content. Separation of concerns is
121	  explicit at the file boundary, not buried inside a multi-author file.
122	- **Symmetric with how `update` already handles `.claude/agents/`.** Both
123	  surfaces become "canonical package data poured in place"; `CLAUDE.md`
124	  joins the same model rather than being a special case.
125	
126	Cons:
127	- **Scenario 39 is contradicted.** `update` does overwrite `CLAUDE.md`.
128	  Operators who hand-edited `CLAUDE.md` under the old contract lose
129	  those edits on `update`. A migration story is required (see below).
130	  The recommendation accepts this hit deliberately; the cleaner
131	  ownership story is judged to be worth the cost.
132	- **Four files instead of one.** More bootstrap surface; a new reader
133	  has more files to orient to. Mitigated by `CLAUDE.md` being short and
134	  literally naming each import (e.g., `@.claude/shop/name.md  # the
135	  shop's own name`) so the import graph is self-documenting.
136	- **Depends on Claude Code's `@<filename>` import mechanism** being
137	  stable across consumers (CLI, IDE extension, future agent runtimes).
138	  Same dependency as alternative E.
139	- **Bootstrap parameter capture lives in a file, not in prose.** A
140	  human glancing at `CLAUDE.md` will see only the imports, not the
141	  shop's name directly. They must follow `@.claude/shop/name.md` to
142	  see "shopsystem-product". Mitigated by `CLAUDE.md` placing the
143	  shop-identity imports at the top, so the indirection is visible
144	  immediately.
145	
146	## Recommendation
147	
148	Adopt **alternative F — canonical-managed `CLAUDE.md` with four typed
149	imports**. The contract becomes:
150	
151	1. Canonical primer content for each shop type ships as a dedicated file
152	   in `shop-templates` package data (`lead-primer.md`, `bc-primer.md`),
153	   parallel to the role-prompt template files already shipped there. A
154	   canonical `CLAUDE.md` template body also ships as package data, one
155	   per shop type.
156	2. Bootstrap writes **five** files into the target shop:
157	   - `CLAUDE.md` at the target root (canonical-managed).
158	   - `.claude/shop/name.md` (shop-owned, bootstrap-only) — the literal
159	     value passed as `--shop-name`.
160	   - `.claude/shop/type.md` (shop-owned, bootstrap-only) — the literal
161	     value passed as `--shop-type`.
162	   - `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md` (canonical-managed) —
163	     the canonical router-prompt body.
164	   - `.claude/shop/primer.md` (shop-owned, bootstrap-only placeholder)
165	     — empty or near-empty; the shop fills it in over time.
166	   `CLAUDE.md`'s body imports those four files in a fixed order with
167	   short headings that name each import's role.
168	3. `update` overwrites two files: `CLAUDE.md` (from the canonical
169	   `CLAUDE.md` template for the shop's declared type) and
170	   `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md` (from the canonical primer
171	   for the shop's declared type). It reads `.claude/shop/type.md` to
172	   know which shop type's canonical to apply.
173	4. `update` is idempotent when both canonical-managed files already
174	   match package data byte-for-byte — no diagnostic, no file mtime
175	   change, exit zero.
176	5. `update` does **not** touch `.claude/shop/name.md`,
177	   `.claude/shop/type.md`, or `.claude/shop/primer.md` under any
178	   circumstances.
179	6. **Migration** for legacy shops (including this product's own lead
180	   shop today, whose `CLAUDE.md` predates the four-file split): see the
181	   "Migration" subsection below.
182	
183	### File-path convention
184	
185	The split lives under `.claude/`:
186	
187	- `.claude/canonical/` — **canonical-managed**. Files here are written
188	  by `bootstrap` and rewritten by `update` from package data. Operators
189	  must not hand-edit; edits will be silently reverted on the next
190	  `update`. Today this directory already houses the canonical agent
191	  files (`bc-implementer.md`, etc., via `.claude/agents/` — see note
192	  below); alternative F adds `<shop-type>-primer.md` to its content
193	  set.
194	- `.claude/shop/` — **shop-managed**. Files here are written once at
195	  bootstrap and then owned by the shop. `update` never touches anything
196	  under `.claude/shop/`.
197	- `CLAUDE.md` at the target root — **canonical-managed** (this is what
198	  changes under F). Its content is a deterministic function of
199	  `.claude/shop/type.md` and canonical package data.
200	
201	Note on `.claude/agents/`: today the agent role-prompt files live there
202	and are managed by `update`. Under F they remain where they are;
203	`.claude/agents/` is canonical-managed in the same sense as
204	`.claude/canonical/`. The PDR does not propose renaming `.claude/agents/`
205	into `.claude/canonical/agents/`; that is a follow-up consolidation if
206	desired.
207	
208	Rationale for path choice: ownership is legible from the path alone.
209	`.claude/canonical/...` says "do not hand-edit"; `.claude/shop/...` says
210	"this is yours". A new operator does not need to read a contract
211	document to know which is which.
212	
213	### Migration
214	
215	A legacy shop (one bootstrapped under the pre-F contract) has a
216	monolithic `CLAUDE.md` containing canonical primer text inlined,
217	shop-authored prose interleaved, and no `.claude/shop/` directory.
218	This product's own lead shop is exactly such a legacy shop today, so
219	the migration UX is concrete, not hypothetical.
220	
221	The recommended migration shape is **refuse-with-diagnostic** (not
222	auto-migrate). When `update` runs in a target that lacks
223	`.claude/shop/type.md`, it:
224	
225	- Exits non-zero.
226	- Writes a diagnostic to stderr naming the migration steps the
227	  operator must perform: create `.claude/shop/{name,type,primer}.md`
228	  with appropriate contents; move any shop-authored prose from the
229	  existing `CLAUDE.md` into `.claude/shop/primer.md`; then re-run
230	  `update`.
231	- Touches no files in the target.
232	
233	Rationale: auto-migrate would have to parse a free-form `CLAUDE.md` to
234	separate canonical text from shop-authored prose, with no reliable
235	delimiter. That's exactly the brittleness alternative D was rejected
236	for. Refuse-with-diagnostic keeps the migration step explicit, manual,
237	and reviewable; the cost is a one-time per-shop operator action.
238	
239	A future `shop-templates migrate` subcommand could automate the file
240	moves once we accept the legacy `CLAUDE.md` will need a sentinel or
241	operator-marked region to make the split deterministic. That is out of
242	scope for this PDR.
243	
244	## Scenarios this PDR contradicts
245	
246	- **39** (`update-preserves-shop-owned-claude-md-edits`). Under F,
247	  `update` overwrites `CLAUDE.md` from canonical. The original
248	  invariant ("byte-for-byte preservation of `CLAUDE.md`") no longer
249	  holds against `CLAUDE.md` itself. The replacement invariant is that
250	  `update` preserves shop-authored content **in
251	  `.claude/shop/primer.md`** byte-for-byte; `CLAUDE.md` itself is
252	  canonical-managed and outside the preservation contract. The PO
253	  proposes **amending** scenario 39 (not superseding) — the same
254	  identifier should continue to express "update does not destroy the
255	  shop's authored content," with the storage location updated to
256	  `.claude/shop/primer.md`. Architect's call whether amend or supersede.
257	
258	## Scenarios this PDR preserves (unchanged)
259	
260	- **31** (bootstrap writes top-level `CLAUDE.md`) — preserved.
261	  `bootstrap` continues to write `CLAUDE.md`; the file is non-empty
262	  (its body contains the four import lines plus a short header).
263	- **44** (canonical primer is package data) — preserved. The canonical
264	  primer continues to ship as package data, now as dedicated
265	  `<shop-type>-primer.md` files. A second canonical asset
266	  (`<shop-type>-claude-md.md` or similar — the canonical `CLAUDE.md`
267	  template body) is added; this is additive to 44, not a contradiction.
268	- **56** (`update-rejects-missing-target-with-argparse-error`) — the
269	  error-path clause that `CLAUDE.md` is unchanged from before the
270	  invocation **holds**, because `update` writes nothing on argparse
271	  error. The wording can stand verbatim; on the success path,
272	  `CLAUDE.md` may now change, but the error-path invariant is
273	  unrelated to the success-path contract.
274	- **40, 41** (`.gitignore` and `.beads/` non-touch invariants) —
275	  unaffected; F changes only the `CLAUDE.md` surface.
276	
277	## Scenarios this PDR amends (Architect call)
278	
279	- **45** (`bootstrap-generated-claude-md-names-the-shops-identity`).
280	  Under F, the substring check `that file contains the literal substring
281	  "<shop_name>"` no longer passes against `CLAUDE.md` directly — the
282	  shop name lives in `.claude/shop/name.md`, imported by `CLAUDE.md`.
283	  Two phrasings, Architect's pick:
284	  - **Phrasing A (PO leans):** retain the assertion at the level of
285	    the assistant-resolved `CLAUDE.md` (imports included). The
286	    scenario pins "the shop's identity is named in the agent's
287	    startup context," which is what we actually care about.
288	  - **Phrasing B:** amend the scenario to assert "the `CLAUDE.md`
289	    import graph names the shop identity" — i.e., `CLAUDE.md` imports
290	    `.claude/shop/name.md`, and that file contains `<shop_name>`.
291	    More mechanism-precise; couples the scenario to the import
292	    discipline.
293	  PO leans toward Phrasing A.
294	- **71 / 72 / 73** (Monitor-arming directive in canonical and
295	  bootstrap-generated `CLAUDE.md`). Same dichotomy as in v2 of this
296	  PDR. Under F, the directive lives in
297	  `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md`, reachable from
298	  `CLAUDE.md` via import. Phrasing A (assertion at the level of
299	  assistant-observable behavior — the directive is present in the
300	  agent's startup context) is preserved as-is. Phrasing B (assert the
301	  storage location directly) requires amendment to name the canonical
302	  primer file. PO leans Phrasing A — the scenarios pin agent-
303	  observable behavior, not storage location.
304	
305	## New scenarios this PDR will author after acceptance
306	
307	1. **Canonical `CLAUDE.md` body ships as package data, per shop type.**
308	   `shop-templates` exposes a canonical `CLAUDE.md` template body for
309	   each shop type via the same package-data surface as the primer and
310	   role-prompt templates. (Companion to scenario 44.)
311	2. **Bootstrap writes the canonical-managed `CLAUDE.md`** whose body
312	   is the canonical template body for the shop's type, byte-for-byte.
313	3. **Bootstrap writes `.claude/shop/name.md`** containing exactly the
314	   value passed as `--shop-name`, with no other content.
315	4. **Bootstrap writes `.claude/shop/type.md`** containing exactly the
316	   value passed as `--shop-type` (`bc` or `lead`), with no other content.
317	5. **Bootstrap writes `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md`** with
318	   the canonical primer for the chosen shop type, byte-for-byte from
319	   package data.
320	6. **Bootstrap writes `.claude/shop/primer.md`** as a shop-authored
321	   placeholder (empty or near-empty); the operator may populate it
322	   later. (Companion to "shop-authored prose has a dedicated home"
323	   under F.)
324	7. **`CLAUDE.md` body imports the four typed files in a fixed order**
325	   — property-level assertion: when the assistant resolves `CLAUDE.md`,
326	   the contents of all four files are present in its startup context.
327	   Literal import syntax is an Implementer follow-up against
328	   authoritative Claude Code documentation.
329	8. **`update` overwrites `CLAUDE.md`** with the canonical `CLAUDE.md`
330	   body for the shop's declared type when the target's `CLAUDE.md`
331	   has drifted from canonical.
332	9. **`update` overwrites `.claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md`**
333	   when the canonical primer has drifted from the target's copy.

335	    circumstances on the success path.
336	11. **`update` does not touch `.claude/shop/type.md`** under any
337	    circumstances on the success path.

339	    circumstances on the success path (this is the F-shaped
340	    replacement for the old scenario 39's intent — shop-authored
341	    content is preserved, just in a different file).
342	13. **`update` is idempotent** when both canonical-managed files
343	    already match package data byte-for-byte — exit zero, no
344	    diagnostic, no file mtime change.
345	14. **`update` reads `.claude/shop/type.md`** to determine which
346	    shop type's canonical templates to apply. (Pins the data flow:
347	    the shop's declared type at bootstrap is authoritative for
348	    subsequent updates, not a flag re-passed at update time.)
349	15. **Migration scenario — refuse-with-diagnostic.** When `update`
350	    runs against a target that lacks `.claude/shop/type.md` (legacy
351	    bootstrap), `update` exits non-zero, writes a diagnostic naming
352	    the migration steps the operator must perform, and touches no
353	    files in the target.
```
